mnemonics.title: KERNEL Remember Kernel
mnemonics.content: KERNEL: Keep Everything that maps to Zero (the zero vector)

mnemonics.title: LINEAR Properties
mnemonics.content: L-I-N-E-A-R: Linear functions satisfy Invariance (addition) and Non-zero homogeneity - preserves Vector operations, Additive, Real homogeneity

acronyms.expansion: Linear Time-Invariant (systems in signal processing based on linear transformations)
acronyms.usage: Used in control systems and signal processing where linear transformations satisfy time-invariance property

acronyms.expansion: System of Equations - solved using linear transformations represented as matrices
acronyms.usage: Ax = b can be viewed as linear transformation T(x) = b where T(x) = Ax

visualTips.visualization: Think of kernel as a 'funnel' - all vectors collapsing to the single point 0. Draw a mapping diagram where multiple arrows point TO the zero vector, showing how they 'vanish'.
Imagine shining a flashlight (transformation) on the domain - the illuminated area on the target is the range. Some points may remain dark if transformation isn't onto.
Think of matrix A as a 'machine' that takes column vector x and produces new vector. Each column of A tells where one basis vector lands.

analogies.analogy: Like a factory machine: input raw materials (vector), transform them through fixed rules (linear transformation), get finished products (output vector). The rules never change - always the same transformation.
Kernel is like a special eraser that turns certain vectors into nothing (zero). These are the vectors that 'disappear' under the transformation.
Think of domain as a pool of water. Rank is water reaching the destination (range), nullity is water 'lost' or 'absorbed' (kernel). Total always stays the same.
